element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation mechanic; player learns object interaction with no timing pressure
Teaches timing and positioning; moderate skill requirement
Active manipulation of possessed objects; adds projectile mechanics
Resource management pressure; creates incentive for efficient scaring

element_introduction_order.element: Underworld hazards (bats, bouncing skulls, disembodied hands)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Direct combat avoidance; health is finite and doesn't regenerate between underworld stages
Combat encounter; appears after each house is cleared
Temporary power-ups found mid-underworld; disappear quickly, require speed to claim
Passive environmental threat; accelerates ecto tank depletion

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully frightening a family member (partial ecto recovery)
Exiting underworld stage via found exit
Collecting rare star-shaped full-heal ecto drop in underworld
Completing a house (transition to next area)
typical_tension_triggers: Ecto tank gradually depleting during house exploration
Dog bark damage accelerating resource drain
Entry into underworld with hazards present
Health portrait shrinking in underworld from hazard hits
Ecto Beast encounter after house completion
Spells appearing and disappearing quickly in underworld
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dual-phase cycle: house exploration with passive resource drain creates slow-burn tension, punctuated by underworld stages with active hazards and health loss. Relief cycles through successful scares and exits. Escalates across four houses as family becomes harder to frighten.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Approximately 400 possessable objects distributed across four houses; not all objects required to complete a level, allowing optional deeper exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: House 1 introduces all three Fright 'em types in single-family-member scenarios; ecto tank still relatively forgiving; underworld hazards minimal initially
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Houses 2-3 escalate family member count and behavioral complexity; ecto drain accelerates; underworld hazards increase in frequency and damage severity
difficulty_curve.late_game: House 4 final challenge with full family present; multiple Ecto Beast encounters; twelve underworld dungeon paths with varied hazard configurations
notable_spikes: First underworld entry (new hazard types introduced)
First Ecto Beast encounter (combat requirement shift)
Transition to House 2 (family becomes harder to scare)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of four houses follows: explore → interact with Fright 'ems → accumulate family fear → drive family out → trigger Ecto Beast → return to underworld. Houses increase in complexity but maintain identical mechanical structure.

Resource collection stage with environmental hazards. Player must locate single ecto drops one at a time, navigate to exit with health management, potentially find temporary spell pickups. Direction arrows guide off-screen objectives.
3/4 overhead perspective encourages spatial awareness of object placement and family positioning across connected rooms. Optional backtracking to reuse Fright 'ems on different family members.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Living room → kitchen → bedroom connections within a single house
